message:
fix(db): harden SQL queries for portability and add SQLite corruption auto-recovery

Three areas of database robustness improvement:

1. SQL query decomposition (robot.rs, finalize.rs): Replace complex
   single queries using GROUP_CONCAT, SUM(CASE...), correlated
   subqueries, and multi-table JOINs with simpler sequential queries
   and scalar subqueries. This avoids SQL portability issues across
   SQLite versions and makes each query independently debuggable.
   Add COALESCE guards around json_array_length/json_each calls in
   finalize.rs to handle NULL attachments gracefully.

2. Corruption auto-recovery (pool.rs): Both startup_integrity_check
   and full_integrity_check now attempt ensure_sqlite_file_healthy()
   recovery when DbConn::open_file fails with a corruption error.
   New reinitialize_without_backup function quarantines corrupted
   database files (with .corrupt-YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_mmm suffix) along
   with their WAL/SHM sidecars, then creates a fresh empty database
   as a last-resort fallback.

3. Remove duplicate guard (storage/lib.rs): The empty-path check in
   resolve_archive_relative_path was present twice; remove the
   redundant second occurrence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+                &[Value::BigInt(id)],
+            )
+            .map_err(|e| CliError::Other(format!("outbox recipient fallback query failed: {e}")))?

+            to_rows
+        };
+        let recipient_names = recipient_rows
+            .iter()
+            .filter_map(|r| {
+                r.get_named::<String>("name")

-            .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())

+            .filter(|name| !name.is_empty())
+            .collect::<Vec<_>>()
+            .join(", ");
+        let recipient_names = if recipient_names.is_empty() {
+            "(no recipients)".to_string()
+        } else {
+            recipient_names
+        };
